bindings.quest_action: replace stolen storehouse supplies before the audit
bindings.quest_focus: the food I took from the storehouse to feed starving children
bindings.quest_stakes: exile from the settlement if the theft is discovered
bindings.quest_threat: an investigation is closing in on the broken lock

dialogueChunks.intro: I know what I did was wrong, {player_name}. I'm not here to pretend otherwise. But if you'd seen their faces, you'd have done the same thing.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My neighbor's children hadn't eaten in three days. So I took {quest_focus} to feed them. {target_npc} is leading the investigation into the broken lock. I need {quest_item} to {quest_action}. {quest_time_pressure}, and if the storehouse is full when they check, there's nothing to investigate.
dialogueChunks.outro: Call me a thief. I'll accept it. But {quest_stakes} is a price I'll pay knowing those children ate that night.

playerResponses.text: Stealing to feed hungry children isn't the crime they think it is. I'll help.
You broke the law for the right reasons. Let's fix this before it gets worse.
Theft is theft, no matter the reason. I can't be part of a cover-up.

bindings.quest_action: disguise a deserter's identity and arrange safe passage
bindings.quest_focus: a young soldier who refused to burn an innocent village
bindings.quest_stakes: both the soldier and my sister face hanging if caught
bindings.quest_threat: the garrison is asking questions and closing in
bindings.quest_origin: a monstrous order that no decent person could carry out

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need someone who doesn't know the people involved. Someone who can look at this clearly.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My sister is harboring {quest_focus}: a deserter who refused an order to burn a village suspected of aiding {enemy_type}. {quest_origin}, and the soldier couldn't carry it out. But desertion is a hanging offense, and {target_npc} has been asking questions around {location}. I need {quest_item} to {quest_action} to a settlement {location_hint} where nobody knows their face.
dialogueChunks.outro: The law says hang the deserter. My heart says save {quest_focus}. {quest_stakes}, but I've chosen my heart. Will you help?

playerResponses.text: A soldier with a conscience deserves protection, not a noose. Count me in.
Harboring deserters and forging identities. This is serious. But I'll help.
I won't help a deserter escape, no matter the reason.

bindings.quest_action: gather wild ingredients to keep brewing unlicensed remedies
bindings.quest_focus: the secret medicine I brew for people who can't afford guild prices
bindings.quest_stakes: the sick and poor will go untreated if I'm caught
bindings.quest_threat: the alchemist guild has sent enforcers to find the source
bindings.quest_origin: guild-controlled prices that only the wealthy can afford

dialogueChunks.intro: I've been making medicine for people who can't pay, {player_name}. Sounds noble until you hear the rest.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The alchemist guild controls all healing supplies in the region. {quest_origin}, so I've been secretly brewing {quest_focus} and distributing it to the poor. {quest_threat}, and {target_npc} suspects me. I need {quest_item} from the highlands {location_hint} to {quest_action}. The {enemy_type} up there make it dangerous, but people in {location} are counting on me.
dialogueChunks.outro: Healing the sick shouldn't be a crime. But {quest_stakes}, and I won't stop. I can't.

playerResponses.text: Medicine for those who need it, laws be damned. What do you need?
Contraband compassion. I like you. Tell me where the herbs grow.
Going against a guild with enforcers is a fight I'd rather avoid.
